[Page 1]
Project Report: ACI.Agent — RivalRadar
AutonomousCompetitive IntelligenceAgent
Date:April24,2026
Preparedby: BackendDeveloper
ProjectName:ACI.Agent(RivalRadar)
Status:Week2Completed—Week3InProgress
1. Summary
ACI.Agent (RivalRadar) is a full-stack, AI-powered Autonomous Competitive Intelligence system built
on.NET Core 10and Angular 17+. The systemeliminates theneed for manual competitor researchby
deploying a team of specialized AI agents that autonomously collect, analyze, and synthesize

deploying a team of specialized AI agents that autonomously collect, analyze, and synthesize
competitive signals from multiple online sources — delivering structured weekly intelligence reports to
stakeholdersviaemail.
BusinessValue:
• Efficiency:Eliminates5-10hoursperweekofmanualcompetitorresearchthroughfully
autonomousagentruns.
• Accuracy:LeveragesRetrieval-AugmentedGeneration(RAG)togroundAIreportsinreal,
collectedsignaldata—minimizinghallucinations.
• Scalability:Themulti-agentarchitectureallowsnewdatasourcestobeaddedasindividualagent
fileswithouttouchingexistingcode.

fileswithouttouchingexistingcode.
Current Work: The backend is fully complete — all 5 agents operational, AI synthesis working, and
weeklyreportsbeingdeliveredviaemail.TheAngularfrontend(Week3)isnowinprogress.
2. Problem Statement
Every product team, founder, and sales manager needs to know what their competitors are doing.
Today,trackingcompetitoractivityisdonemanually—checkingwebsites,scrollingjobboards,reading
reviews, googling for news. It takes hours every week, happens inconsistently, and insights always
arrivetoolatetoacton.
LimitationsofManualApproaches:

[Page 2]
• NoSynthesis:Rawdataiscollectedbutneverconnected—patternsandimplicationsgo
unnoticed.
• DelayedInsights:Bythetimecompetitiveintelligencereachesthestrategyteam,itisalready
stale.
3. Solution Overview
ThesolutionimplementsaMulti-AgentAutonomousSystemusing.NETCore10.Thecorephilosophy
istomovebeyondsimpleAPIcallsandinsteaddeployspecializedagents—eachwithonefocusedjob
—coordinatedbyanorchestratorthatrunsautomaticallyeveryday.
KeyAgenticAI Features:
• AutonomousDataCollection:5specializedagentsrundaily—WebWatcher,JobBoard,RSS
Feed,Reddit, HackerNews—eachmonitoringaspecificsignalsource.

• AutomatedDelivery:ReportsaregeneratedeveryMondayat9:00AManddeliveredasstyled
HTMLemailsviaGmailSMTP.
4. Technical Architecture
Thesystemfollowsaclean3-layerarchitecturewithclearseparationofconcerns:
• APILayer(ACI.Agent.API):RESTcontrollers,Program.cs,appsettings,SwaggerUI,Hangfire
dashboardconfiguration.
• BALLayer(ACI.Agent.BAL):Allbusinesslogic—Agents,Services,Interfaces,DTOs,Helpers,
Scheduler,OllamaService,VectorMemoryService.
• DALLayer(ACI.Agent.DAL):Entities,ApplicationDbContext,Repositoryinterfacesand
implementationsforall5tables.
AgentPipelineFlow:

[Page 3]
• Step4:EveryMondayat9:00AM—SynthesisAgentreadssignals,embedsintoQdrant,
retrieveshistory,callsOllamaLLM.
• Step5:ReportAgentparsesAIoutput,savesWeeklyReporttoPostgreSQL,sendsHTMLemail
viaGmailSMTP.
5. Tech Stack
Component Technology Rationale
BackendAPI ASP.NETCore10 RESTAPI,dependencyinjection,clean3-layerarchitecture.
HeadlessChrome—handlesJavaScript-heavymodern
WebScraping MicrosoftPlaywright
websites.
Database PostgreSQL+EFCore10 Structuredstorageforcompetitors,signals,logs,reports.
VectorDatabase QdrantCloud(freetier) Semanticsignalmemory—768-dimvectorswithpayload
indexing.

VectorDatabase QdrantCloud(freetier) Semanticsignalmemory—768-dimvectorswithpayload
indexing.
LLM(Chat) Ollamagemma4:e4b AIreasoningviasharedserverat192.168.1.240:11434.
Embeddings Ollamanomic-embed-text Text-to-vectorconversionvialocalOllamaatlocalhost:11434.
Scheduler Hangfire+SQLite Backgroundjobs—dailyagentrunsandweeklyreport
generation.
Email GmailSMTP HTMLreportdeliveryviaSystem.Net.Mail—100%free.
DashboardUI—competitormanagement,signals,reports,
Frontend Angular17+/PrimeNG
agentlogs.
6. Features Implemented (Current State)

Frontend Angular17+/PrimeNG
agentlogs.
6. Features Implemented (Current State)
• Multi-SourceAgentSystem:5specializedagents—WebWatcher,JobBoard,RSSFeed,Reddit,
HackerNews—alloperationalandtested.
• SmartChangeDetection:WebWatcherusesword-leveldiffpercentage(threshold5%)toavoid
noisesignalsfromminorpageupdates.
• DB-BackedPageCache:PreviouspagesnapshotsstoredinPostgreSQL—changedetection
survivesapplicationrestarts.
• StructuredSignalStorage:EverysignalstoredwithAgentType,SignalType,ImportanceScore
(1-3),ConfidenceScore,SourceUrl,andOccurredAt.

[Page 4]
• EmailReportDelivery:StyledHTMLreportdeliveredviaGmailSMTP—confirmedworkingand
receivedininbox.
• HangfireScheduler:Tworecurringjobs—dailyagentrunat8:00AMandweeklyreport
generationeveryMondayat9:00AM.
• FullRESTAPI:14endpointscoveringcompetitors,signals,agentruns,andreportgeneration—
documentedinSwaggerUI.
7. Concepts Covered
ThisprojectservesasacomprehensivepracticalimplementationofmodernAgenticAIprinciples:
• Retrieval-AugmentedGeneration(RAG):Signalsembeddedasvectors—LLMgroundedinreal
collecteddata,nottrainingmemory.

• AutonomousScheduling:Hangfiremanagesagentlifecycle—nohumantriggerrequiredafter
initialconfiguration.
• PromptEngineering:StructuredoutputformatenforcedinLLMprompt—SUMMARY,KEY
INSIGHTS,STRATEGICIMPLICATIONS,RECOMMENDATIONS.
• ObservabilityPattern:Everyagentruncreatesatraceablelogentry—fullaudittrailofwhatran,
when,andwhatitfound.
• SoftDeletePattern:Competitorsdeactivatedinsteadofdeleted—historicalsignalsandreports
preservedpermanently.

9. Remaining Work
1.AngularFrontendDashboard(Week3):
Building the complete Angular 17+ UI with PrimeNG — dashboard with stat cards, competitor

Building the complete Angular 17+ UI with PrimeNG — dashboard with stat cards, competitor
management with CRUD, signal feed with filters, report viewer with accordion layout, and agent
observabilitypanelshowingrunlogsandstatus.
2.JWTAuthentication&Authorization(Week4):
ImplementingsecureloginwithJWTtokens,role-basedaccesscontrol(AdminvsViewer),andAngular
routeguardstoprotectalldashboardpages.
3.ManualAgentTriggerfromUI(Week4):
Addingtheabilityforuserstotriggeragentrunsondemandfromthedashboard—percompetitororfor
allcompetitors—withreal-timestatusfeedbackviatoastnotifications.

allcompetitors—withreal-timestatusfeedbackviatoastnotifications.
4.EmbeddingQualityImprovement(Ongoing):
Verifying nomic-embed-text embeddings are correctly stored in Qdrant with 768 dimensions. Testing
filteredsemanticsearchpercompetitortoensurehistoricalcontextisaccurateandrelevant.
5.FinalTesting&DemoPreparation(Week4):
End-to-end testing of the full system — agents to signals to report to email. Bug fixes, UI polish,
architecturedocumentation,andarecorded3-minutedemowalkthroughfortheportfolio.

11. Conclusion
ACI.Agent (RivalRadar) successfully demonstrates the transition from simple web scrapers to a fully
autonomous,AI-poweredcompetitiveintelligencesystem.Thebackendisproduction-ready—5agents
collecting real signals daily, AI synthesizing those signals into structured reports, and email delivery
confirmed working end-to-end. The current system covers all 8 phases of the Agentic AI learning
roadmap— LLM fundamentals, embeddings andRAG, observability, tool calling, agent loops, memory

roadmap— LLM fundamentals, embeddings andRAG, observability, tool calling, agent loops, memory
systems, multi-agent coordination, and framework concepts — making it both a strong portfolio project
and a deep hands-on learning experience. With the Angular frontend (Week 3) and final polish (Week
4)remaining,theprojectisontrackforacomplete.
Digico Internal Deployment & Image Sharing Guide
This document explains all activities that must be performed by the Digico team before sharing the project with the customer.
Access to Digico Azure Container Registry
Repository/images already pushed to Digico ACR
5. Create Scope Map for Single Repository

az acr scope-map create \
--name customerScopeMap \
--registry <digico-acr-name> \
--repository <repository-name> content/read
6. Create Scope Map for Multiple Repositories

az acr scope-map create \
--name customerScopeMap \
--registry <digico-acr-name> \
--repository frontend-app content/read \
--repository backend-api content/read \
--repository document-analyzer content/read

az acr token create \
--name customerToken \
--registry <digico-acr-name> \
--scope-map customerScopeMap

az acr token credential generate \
--name customerToken \
--registry <digico-acr-name>

az acr network-rule add \
--name digicoregistry \
--ip-address <ip-1>

az acr network-rule add \
--name digicoregistry \
--ip-address <ip-2>
Repository images available in Digico ACR
LLM stands for Large Language Model.
A Large Language Model is an AI system trained on massive amounts of text to predict the next token (word or part of a word) in a sequence.
The capital of France is Paris
But it doesn't know facts like humans do.
It only predicts statistical patterns learned from training data.
LLMs are trained on huge datasets including:
Because of this training, they learn patterns like:
Write a Python function to sort a list
The model predicts a pattern that usually follows such instructions:
Because LLMs predict probability, not truth.
Give a citation for this paper:
If the paper doesn't exist, the model may still generate something like:
Smith et al., Journal of AI Research, 2034
It happens because the model tries to complete patterns, not verify facts.
An AI agent loop looks like this:
This pattern is called ReAct reasoning.
Practical code to perform testing on above learned LLM behaviour
These parameters control how the model chooses the next token.
Remember: LLMs work using the idea of next-token prediction inside the Transformer Architecture used in modern systems studied in Natural Language Processing.
Temperature controls randomness in token selection.
Top-p controls how many possible tokens the model considers.
The model only considers tokens until probability reaches 90% cumulative.
Practical code to understand temperature and top-p behaviour :
Token and Context Window in LLM :
LLMs do not read text as words.
Tokenization is part of how models based on the
Transformer Architecture process language in the field of Natural Language Processing.
The context window is measured in tokens.
The context window is the maximum tokens the model can process at once.
All must fit inside the context window.
This is one of the most common errors in agent systems.
Designing inputs so the model produces reliable and useful outputs.
Since models based on the Transformer Architecture generate text based on context,      the prompt strongly influences the output.
Most modern LLM prompts follow this structure:
This structure improves consistency and accuracy.
You assign the model a persona or expertise.
Chain-of-Thought prompting makes the LLM reason step-by-step before giving the final answer.
Correct — but we don't know the reasoning.
Modern systems often hide chain-of-thought internally for safety.
Instead of exposing it, they use internal reasoning.
But conceptually the model is still doing:
Tool calling allows the LLM to decide when to call external functions.
But with tool calling they can.
Example of how ReAct with tool calling works
You are an AI agent that MUST use tools to solve problems.
- If a calculation is required, you MUST use the calculate tool.
- If time is requested, use get_time.
After receiving observation you must continue reasoning.
When you know the final answer respond with:
if not all(c in allowed for c in expression):
if action and action in TOOLS:
return "Error: Could not parse action or unknown tool"
result = react_agent("What time is it and what’s 100 + 50?")
Embeddings are numerical vector representations of text, images, or data.
They capture semantic meaning, not just keywords.
Similar meanings → vectors are closer in space.
A database designed to store and query high-dimensional vectors.
Finds closest vectors to a query embedding.
Used to retrieve relevant chunks in RAG.
Splitting large documents into smaller pieces (chunks).
Chunk size: ~200–500 tokens (depends on use case)
Use overlap (10–20%) to avoid losing context

[Page 1]
Agentic AI Roadmap
Phase 1 — LLM Fundamentals
🎯 Goal: Understand how LLMs think and behave.
Core Topics
● What is an LLM?
● Transformer architecture (high-level understanding)
● Tokens & context window
● Temperature & Top-p
● Prompt engineering basics
● Role prompting
● Chain-of-Thought (conceptual understanding)
● Structured output (JSON)
● Function calling concept
● Hallucination behavior
Phase 2 — Embeddings & RAG
🎯 Goal: Understand memory & grounded knowledge retrieval.
Core Topics
● What are embeddings?
● Vector DB
● Vector similarity search
● Cosine similarity

Core Topics
● What are embeddings?
● Vector DB
● Vector similarity search
● Cosine similarity
● Chunking strategy
● RAG architecture
● Retrieval quality considerations
● Citation enforcement
● Grounding strictly to documents

[Page 2]
Tools
● Pinecone
● Weaviate
● Chroma
● Milvus
Phase 3 — Evaluation & Observability
🎯 Goal: Measure quality instead of guessing.
Core Topics
● Prompt versioning
● A/B testing prompts
● Retrieval precision
● Logging LLM inputs/outputs
● Token usage tracking
● Latency monitoring
● Cost tracking
Phase 4 — Tool Calling & Guarded
Execution
🎯 Goal: Enable safe action-taking.
Core Topics
● Tool-augmented LLM architecture
● JSON schema design
● Tool selection reasoning
● Deterministic validation layer
● Human-in-the-loop approval
● Safe execution boundaries

[Page 3]
Phase 5 — Agent Loop & Autonomous
Reasoning
🎯 Goal: Understand controlled autonomy.
Core Topics
● ReAct pattern (Reason + Act)
● Plan → Execute → Observe loop
● Reflection loop
● Self-correction strategies
● Task decomposition
● Retry & fallback logic
Phase 6 — Memory Systems
🎯 Goal: Build persistent intelligent agents.
Types of Memory
● Short-term memory (context window)
● Long-term memory (vector DB)
● Episodic memory
● Semantic memory
● Task history storage
● Reflection storage
Phase 7 — Multi-Agent Systems
🎯 Goal: Understand coordination and specialization.

[Page 4]
Concepts
● Planner agent
● Executor agent
● Critic agent
● Research agent
● Communication protocol
● Shared memory
Phase 8 — Agentic Frameworks
🎯 Goal: Use frameworks strategically, not blindly.
● LangGraph (structured orchestration)
● Microsoft AutoGen (enterprise-style multi-agent)
● CrewAI (collaborative agents)
Note:
● This roadmap covers the main topics required to learn Agentic AI.
● Some topics might be missing right now due to current knowledge gaps.
● Any missing concepts will be picked up and added during the learning process.

● Any missing concepts will be picked up and added during the learning process.
● Along with theory, we will build a few basic projects to deepen the knowledge.

[Page 1]
CHATBOT EVALUATION
Q&A Test Suite
A Comprehensive Question Bank for AI Chatbot Benchmarking
Total Categories 13
Total Questions 91 (7 per category)
Format Question + One-Line Expected Answer
Purpose Chatbot Response Quality Testing
Coverage Politics, Reasoning, Math, Science, History & more
HOW TO USE THIS DOCUMENT
1. Use each question to prompt your chatbot and record its response.
2. Compare the chatbot answer with the Expected Answer provided.
3. Score the response for accuracy, completeness, and clarity.
4. Identify weak categories where your chatbot underperforms.

01. nn Politics
Q01 n QUESTION
What is the difference between a democracy and a republic?
EXPECTED ANSWER
A democracy gives citizens direct power to make decisions, while a republic uses elected
representatives to govern on behalf of the people.
Q02 n QUESTION
What is the role of the United Nations Security Council?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The UN Security Council is responsible for maintaining international peace and security, and can
authorize military action or impose sanctions.
Q03 n QUESTION
What does the term 'geopolitics' mean?
EXPECTED ANSWER

authorize military action or impose sanctions.
Q03 n QUESTION
What does the term 'geopolitics' mean?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Geopolitics studies how geography, power, and international relations influence a country's foreign policy
and global influence.
Q04 n QUESTION
What is a coalition government?
EXPECTED ANSWER
A coalition government is formed when multiple political parties cooperate to form a majority and share
power in governance.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is the significance of the G20 summit?
EXPECTED ANSWER

power in governance.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is the significance of the G20 summit?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The G20 summit brings together leaders of the world's 20 largest economies to discuss global economic
policy, trade, and cooperation.
Q06 n QUESTION
What is the concept of 'separation of powers'?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Separation of powers divides government into three branches — legislative, executive, and judicial — to
prevent any one branch from gaining too much power.
Q07 n QUESTION
What is martial law?
EXPECTED ANSWER

prevent any one branch from gaining too much power.
Q07 n QUESTION
What is martial law?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Martial law is the temporary imposition of military rule over a civilian population, usually during
emergencies or civil unrest.
02. n Reasoning & Logic

[Page 4]
Q01 n QUESTION
If all mammals are warm-blooded, and a whale is a mammal, what can we conclude?
EXPECTED ANSWER
We can conclude that a whale is warm-blooded — this is a classic deductive reasoning (syllogism)
example.
Q02 n QUESTION
A bat and a ball cost $1.10 together. The bat costs $1 more than the ball. How much does the
ball cost?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The ball costs $0.05 (5 cents); the bat costs $1.05, and together they total $1.10.
Q03 n QUESTION
What logical fallacy is it when someone dismisses an argument because of who said it, not
what was said?
EXPECTED ANSWER

what was said?
EXPECTED ANSWER
This is called the Ad Hominem fallacy — attacking the person rather than addressing their argument.
Q04 n QUESTION
If it rains, the ground is wet. The ground is wet. Does it mean it rained?
EXPECTED ANSWER
No — this is the fallacy of affirming the consequent; the ground could be wet for other reasons like a
sprinkler.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is the difference between inductive and deductive reasoning?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Deductive reasoning draws specific conclusions from general rules, while inductive reasoning builds
general conclusions from specific observations.

general conclusions from specific observations.
Q06 n QUESTION
You have 3 boxes: one has only apples, one has only oranges, one has both. All labels are
wrong. You pick one fruit from one box. How do you label all correctly?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Pick from the box labeled 'Both' — since all labels are wrong, it must be either apples or oranges only;
use that result to correctly label all three boxes.
Q07 n QUESTION
What is Occam's Razor?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Occam's Razor is the principle that among competing explanations, the simplest one with fewest
assumptions is usually correct.

03. n Mathematics
Q01 n QUESTION
What is the value of Pi (p) up to five decimal places?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Pi is approximately 3.14159.
Q02 n QUESTION
What is the sum of interior angles of a triangle?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The sum of interior angles of any triangle is always 180 degrees.
Q03 n QUESTION
What is the difference between permutation and combination?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Permutation considers order important (arrangements), while combination does not consider order
(selections).
Q04 n QUESTION
Solve: If 2x + 5 = 15, what is x?
EXPECTED ANSWER

(selections).
Q04 n QUESTION
Solve: If 2x + 5 = 15, what is x?
EXPECTED ANSWER
x = 5 (subtract 5 from both sides to get 2x = 10, then divide by 2).
Q05 n QUESTION
What is the Pythagorean theorem?
EXPECTED ANSWER
In a right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of squares of the other two sides: a^2 +
b^2 = c^2.
Q06 n QUESTION
What is a prime number? Give three examples.
EXPECTED ANSWER
A prime number has only two factors: 1 and itself — examples are 2, 3, 5, 7, and 11.
Q07 n QUESTION
What is the probability of getting heads twice in a row when flipping a fair coin?

Q07 n QUESTION
What is the probability of getting heads twice in a row when flipping a fair coin?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The probability is 1/4 (0.25 or 25%) since each flip is independent with a 1/2 chance.
04. n Controversial Topics
Q01 n QUESTION
Should artificial intelligence be regulated by governments?
EXPECTED ANSWER
This is debated: proponents say regulation ensures safety and ethics, while opponents argue it may stifle
innovation and competitiveness.

[Page 6]
Q02 n QUESTION
Is social media more harmful than beneficial to society?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Opinions are divided: benefits include connectivity and information access, while harms include
misinformation, mental health impacts, and addiction.
Q03 n QUESTION
Should the death penalty be abolished globally?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Supporters of abolition cite human rights and wrongful convictions; opponents argue it serves as a
deterrent and delivers justice for heinous crimes.
Q04 n QUESTION
Is universal basic income (UBI) a practical economic policy?
EXPECTED ANSWER

Q04 n QUESTION
Is universal basic income (UBI) a practical economic policy?
EXPECTED ANSWER
UBI advocates argue it reduces poverty and inequality; critics warn of inflation, high costs, and reduced
work incentives.
Q05 n QUESTION
Should genetic engineering of human embryos be allowed?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Supporters highlight potential to eliminate inherited diseases; opponents raise ethical concerns about
'designer babies' and unintended consequences.
Q06 n QUESTION
Is cryptocurrency a reliable alternative to traditional banking?
EXPECTED ANSWER

Q06 n QUESTION
Is cryptocurrency a reliable alternative to traditional banking?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Crypto offers decentralization and financial access, but critics point to extreme volatility, environmental
impact, and lack of regulation.
Q07 n QUESTION
Should junk food be taxed heavily to reduce obesity?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Health advocates support a junk food tax to discourage unhealthy eating, while critics call it regressive
and an overreach of government authority.

05. n Technical News
Q01 n QUESTION
What is a Large Language Model (LLM)?
EXPECTED ANSWER
An LLM is a deep learning model trained on massive text data to understand and generate human-like
language, such as GPT-4 or Claude.
Q02 n QUESTION
What is quantum computing and how is it different from classical computing?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Quantum computing uses qubits that can exist in multiple states simultaneously (superposition), enabling
far faster processing of certain complex problems than classical bits.
Q03 n QUESTION
What does 'open source' mean in software development?
EXPECTED ANSWER

Q03 n QUESTION
What does 'open source' mean in software development?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Open source means the source code of software is freely available for anyone to view, modify, and
distribute.
Q04 n QUESTION
What is edge computing?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Edge computing processes data closer to where it is generated (on devices or local servers) rather than
sending it all to a central cloud, reducing latency.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is the difference between machine learning and deep learning?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Machine learning uses algorithms to learn from data; deep learning is a subset of ML that uses

EXPECTED ANSWER
Machine learning uses algorithms to learn from data; deep learning is a subset of ML that uses
multi-layered neural networks for complex pattern recognition.
Q06 n QUESTION
What is a zero-day vulnerability?
EXPECTED ANSWER
A zero-day vulnerability is a software flaw that is unknown to the vendor and has no patch, making it
immediately exploitable by attackers.
Q07 n QUESTION
What is WebAssembly (WASM)?
EXPECTED ANSWER
WebAssembly is a binary instruction format that allows high-performance applications written in
languages like C or Rust to run in web browsers.

languages like C or Rust to run in web browsers.
06. n Current Affairs

[Page 8]
Q01 n QUESTION
What is the significance of the Paris Climate Agreement?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The Paris Agreement is an international treaty signed in 2015 where countries commit to limiting global
warming to below 2°C above pre-industrial levels.
Q02 n QUESTION
What is BRICS and which countries are its members?
EXPECTED ANSWER
BRICS is an economic bloc originally comprising Brazil, Russia, India, China, and South Africa, recently
expanded to include more nations including UAE and Egypt.
Q03 n QUESTION
What is the ongoing conflict between Israel and Palestine about?
EXPECTED ANSWER

Q03 n QUESTION
What is the ongoing conflict between Israel and Palestine about?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The conflict involves competing claims over land and statehood in the region, rooted in historical,
religious, and political disputes dating back over a century.
Q04 n QUESTION
What is the global impact of rising interest rates by the US Federal Reserve?
EXPECTED ANSWER
US rate hikes strengthen the dollar, raise borrowing costs globally, impact emerging market debt, and
can slow worldwide economic growth.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is NATO and what is its primary purpose?
EXPECTED ANSWER

can slow worldwide economic growth.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is NATO and what is its primary purpose?
EXPECTED ANSWER
NATO (North Atlantic Treaty Organization) is a military alliance of 32 countries whose primary purpose is
collective defense — an attack on one is an attack on all.
Q06 n QUESTION
What is the significance of India's Chandrayaan-3 mission?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Chandrayaan-3 made India the first country to land near the lunar south pole in 2023, a milestone in
space exploration for discovering water ice resources.
Q07 n QUESTION
What is the global refugee crisis?
EXPECTED ANSWER

Q07 n QUESTION
What is the global refugee crisis?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The global refugee crisis refers to the record number of people (over 100 million) forcibly displaced
worldwide due to war, persecution, and climate change.

07. n History & Geography
Q01 n QUESTION
What was the primary cause of World War I?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria in 1914 was the immediate trigger, set
against deeper causes like nationalism, militarism, and alliance systems.
Q02 n QUESTION
What is the largest country in the world by land area?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Russia is the largest country in the world, covering approximately 17.1 million square kilometers.
Q03 n QUESTION
What was the significance of the Silk Road?
EXPECTED ANSWER

Q03 n QUESTION
What was the significance of the Silk Road?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The Silk Road was an ancient trade network connecting Asia, Europe, and Africa, enabling the exchange
of goods, cultures, religions, and ideas.
Q04 n QUESTION
Where is the Amazon River located and why is it important?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The Amazon River is in South America (primarily Brazil) and is the world's largest river by discharge,
supporting the planet's most biodiverse rainforest.
Q05 n QUESTION
What were the causes and consequences of the Industrial Revolution?
EXPECTED ANSWER

Q05 n QUESTION
What were the causes and consequences of the Industrial Revolution?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The Industrial Revolution (18th-19th century) was driven by mechanization and coal power; it
transformed economies, created urban centers, and led to modern capitalism.
Q06 n QUESTION
Which country is known as the 'Land of the Rising Sun' and why?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Japan is called the Land of the Rising Sun because its name in Japanese (Nihon) means 'origin of the
sun,' as it is east of China where the sun appears to rise first.
Q07 n QUESTION
What was the Cold War and what ended it?

Q07 n QUESTION
What was the Cold War and what ended it?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The Cold War was a geopolitical rivalry between the US and USSR from 1947 to 1991; it ended with the
dissolution of the Soviet Union in December 1991.
08. n Science & Technology

[Page 10]
Q01 n QUESTION
What is CRISPR and what is it used for?
EXPECTED ANSWER
CRISPR is a gene-editing technology that allows scientists to precisely cut and modify DNA sequences,
used for treating genetic diseases and research.
Q02 n QUESTION
What is the theory of relativity proposed by Einstein?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Einstein's theory of relativity includes Special Relativity (E=mc², space-time relationship at high speeds)
and General Relativity (gravity as curvature of spacetime).
Q03 n QUESTION
What is the difference between renewable and non-renewable energy?
EXPECTED ANSWER

Q03 n QUESTION
What is the difference between renewable and non-renewable energy?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Renewable energy (solar, wind, hydro) replenishes naturally and is sustainable; non-renewable energy
(coal, oil, gas) is finite and depletes over time.
Q04 n QUESTION
What is Moore's Law?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Moore's Law is the observation that the number of transistors on a microchip doubles approximately
every two years, leading to exponential growth in computing power.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is dark matter?
EXPECTED ANSWER

every two years, leading to exponential growth in computing power.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is dark matter?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Dark matter is a hypothetical form of matter that does not emit or interact with light but exerts
gravitational effects, making up about 27% of the universe.
Q06 n QUESTION
What is the Internet of Things (IoT)?
EXPECTED ANSWER
IoT refers to the network of physical devices (appliances, vehicles, sensors) connected to the internet,
enabling them to collect and exchange data automatically.
Q07 n QUESTION
What is nanotechnology?
EXPECTED ANSWER

enabling them to collect and exchange data automatically.
Q07 n QUESTION
What is nanotechnology?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Nanotechnology is the science of manipulating matter at the atomic and molecular scale (1-100
nanometers) for applications in medicine, materials, and electronics.

[Page 11]
09. n Agriculture
Q01 n QUESTION
What is drip irrigation and why is it efficient?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Drip irrigation delivers water directly to the plant roots through small emitters, reducing water waste by up
to 60% compared to traditional flood irrigation.
Q02 n QUESTION
What is the Green Revolution?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The Green Revolution (1960s-70s) was a period of agricultural transformation where high-yield crop
varieties, fertilizers, and irrigation dramatically increased food production.
Q03 n QUESTION
What is organic farming?
EXPECTED ANSWER

Q03 n QUESTION
What is organic farming?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Organic farming avoids synthetic chemicals and GMOs, using natural methods like composting and crop
rotation to grow food sustainably.
Q04 n QUESTION
What is the role of nitrogen in plant growth?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Nitrogen is essential for plant growth as it is a key component of chlorophyll and proteins, directly
influencing leaf development and photosynthesis.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is crop rotation and what are its benefits?
EXPECTED ANSWER

Q05 n QUESTION
What is crop rotation and what are its benefits?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Crop rotation involves alternating different crops on the same land each season to maintain soil fertility,
reduce pests, and prevent soil erosion.
Q06 n QUESTION
What is precision agriculture?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Precision agriculture uses GPS, sensors, and data analytics to monitor and optimize field-level farming
decisions, maximizing yield while minimizing inputs.
Q07 n QUESTION
What is GM (Genetically Modified) crop technology?
EXPECTED ANSWER

Q07 n QUESTION
What is GM (Genetically Modified) crop technology?
EXPECTED ANSWER
GM crops are plants whose DNA has been altered using genetic engineering to introduce desirable traits
like pest resistance, drought tolerance, or improved nutrition.
10. n Biology

[Page 12]
Q01 n QUESTION
What is the central dogma of molecular biology?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The central dogma states that genetic information flows from DNA to RNA to Protein: DNA is transcribed
to RNA, which is then translated into proteins.
Q02 n QUESTION
What is the difference between mitosis and meiosis?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Mitosis produces two identical diploid cells for growth and repair, while meiosis produces four unique
haploid cells for sexual reproduction.
Q03 n QUESTION
What is natural selection?
EXPECTED ANSWER

haploid cells for sexual reproduction.
Q03 n QUESTION
What is natural selection?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Natural selection is the evolutionary process where organisms with favorable traits survive and
reproduce more successfully, passing those traits to offspring.
Q04 n QUESTION
What is the function of mitochondria in a cell?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Mitochondria are the powerhouse of the cell, producing ATP (energy) through cellular respiration using
oxygen and glucose.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is the difference between DNA and RNA?
EXPECTED ANSWER

oxygen and glucose.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is the difference between DNA and RNA?
EXPECTED ANSWER
DNA is double-stranded, contains thymine, and stores genetic information; RNA is single-stranded,
contains uracil, and helps translate that information into proteins.
Q06 n QUESTION
What is homeostasis?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Homeostasis is the process by which living organisms maintain stable internal conditions (like
temperature and pH) despite changes in the external environment.
Q07 n QUESTION
What is the difference between a virus and a bacterium?
EXPECTED ANSWER

Q07 n QUESTION
What is the difference between a virus and a bacterium?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Bacteria are single-celled living organisms that can reproduce independently; viruses are non-living
particles that must invade host cells to replicate.

[Page 13]
11. nn Chemistry
Q01 n QUESTION
What is the periodic table and who created it?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The periodic table organizes all known chemical elements by atomic number and properties; it was
created by Dmitri Mendeleev in 1869.
Q02 n QUESTION
What is the difference between an acid and a base?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Acids have a pH below 7 and donate protons (H+), while bases have a pH above 7 and accept protons;
water is neutral at pH 7.
Q03 n QUESTION
What is covalent bonding?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Covalent bonding occurs when two atoms share electrons to achieve stable electron configurations,

EXPECTED ANSWER
Covalent bonding occurs when two atoms share electrons to achieve stable electron configurations,
commonly found in molecules like H2O and CO2.
Q04 n QUESTION
What is the law of conservation of mass?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The law of conservation of mass states that matter cannot be created or destroyed in a chemical reaction
— the mass of reactants equals the mass of products.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is the difference between an element and a compound?
EXPECTED ANSWER
An element consists of only one type of atom (e.g., gold, oxygen), while a compound is made of two or

EXPECTED ANSWER
An element consists of only one type of atom (e.g., gold, oxygen), while a compound is made of two or
more different elements chemically bonded (e.g., H2O, NaCl).
Q06 n QUESTION
What is oxidation and reduction (redox)?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Oxidation is the loss of electrons; reduction is the gain of electrons — they always occur together in a
redox reaction, remembered by the acronym OIL RIG.
Q07 n QUESTION
What is an isomer in chemistry?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Isomers are molecules with the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements of atoms,

EXPECTED ANSWER
Isomers are molecules with the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements of atoms,
resulting in different physical or chemical properties.
12. nn Physics

[Page 14]
Q01 n QUESTION
What is Newton's First Law of Motion?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Newton's First Law states that an object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion stays in motion,
unless acted upon by an external unbalanced force.
Q02 n QUESTION
What is the speed of light and why is it important?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately 3 x 10^8 meters per second; it is a universal constant
and the maximum speed at which energy or information can travel.
Q03 n QUESTION
What is the difference between potential energy and kinetic energy?
EXPECTED ANSWER

Q03 n QUESTION
What is the difference between potential energy and kinetic energy?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Potential energy is stored energy based on position (e.g., a rock on a hill), while kinetic energy is the
energy of motion (e.g., the rock rolling down).
Q04 n QUESTION
What is Ohm's Law?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Ohm's Law states that Voltage (V) = Current (I) x Resistance (R), describing the relationship between
these three fundamental electrical quantities.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is the Doppler Effect?
EXPECTED ANSWER

these three fundamental electrical quantities.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is the Doppler Effect?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The Doppler Effect is the change in frequency of a wave (sound or light) as its source moves relative to
the observer — the pitch of an ambulance siren changing as it passes is a common example.
Q06 n QUESTION
What is the Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle?
EXPECTED ANSWER
The Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle states that you cannot simultaneously know the exact position and
momentum of a particle — the more precisely one is known, the less precisely the other can be.
Q07 n QUESTION

momentum of a particle — the more precisely one is known, the less precisely the other can be.
Q07 n QUESTION
What is the difference between nuclear fission and nuclear fusion?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Fission splits heavy atomic nuclei (like uranium) releasing energy — used in nuclear reactors; fusion
combines light nuclei (like hydrogen) releasing far more energy — powers the sun.

[Page 15]
13. nn Verbal & Non-Verbal Communication
Q01 n QUESTION
What is the difference between verbal and non-verbal communication?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Verbal communication uses spoken or written words to convey messages, while non-verbal
communication includes body language, facial expressions, gestures, and tone.
Q02 n QUESTION
What is active listening and why is it important?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Active listening means fully concentrating on, understanding, and responding to a speaker; it builds trust,
reduces misunderstandings, and improves relationships.
Q03 n QUESTION

reduces misunderstandings, and improves relationships.
Q03 n QUESTION
What does the term 'proxemics' mean in communication?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Proxemics is the study of how people use physical space and distance in communication — personal,
social, and public zones convey different levels of intimacy.
Q04 n QUESTION
What is the role of tone of voice in communication?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Tone of voice conveys emotion and intent beyond the words themselves — the same words said in
different tones can mean completely different things.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is 'paralanguage'?
EXPECTED ANSWER

different tones can mean completely different things.
Q05 n QUESTION
What is 'paralanguage'?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Paralanguage refers to vocal cues that accompany speech — including pitch, speed, volume, and
pauses — that add meaning beyond the actual words used.
Q06 n QUESTION
What are Kinesics in non-verbal communication?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Kinesics is the study of body movements — including gestures, posture, and facial expressions — and
how they communicate meaning in interpersonal interactions.
Q07 n QUESTION
What is the 7-38-55 rule in communication?
EXPECTED ANSWER

Q07 n QUESTION
What is the 7-38-55 rule in communication?
EXPECTED ANSWER
Mehrabian's rule states that communication impact is 7% words, 38% tone of voice, and 55% body
language — non-verbal cues dominate emotional communication.

The AI-Powered Promotion & Communication Platform is a full-stack web application designed to help marketing agencies and teams manage marketing campaigns and client communication in one centralized place. It enables users to create and send promotional emails, interact through real-time chat, and leverage advanced Agentic AI for smarter content generation, analysis, and automated responses.
AI-generated email content and campaign materials
Real-time collaborative chat with Agentic AI support
Multi-modal AI — text, image analysis, code handling
Reduce manual content creation time by 60–70%
Improve campaign response quality through AI personalization
Lower operational costs while scaling output
Provide data-driven insights for better marketing decisions
Marketing agencies and teams face significant challenges with traditional approaches:
Time-consuming manual email writing and personalization
Fragmented tools for email, chat, content generation, and campaign tracking
Inconsistent brand voice and quality across campaigns
Slow response times to client queries
Limited ability to analyze images/assets or optimize code/snippets for campaigns
High dependency on human writers — bottlenecks during peak seasons
Inconsistent quality and slow iteration cycles
Poor scalability for growing client bases
No intelligent assistance for creative or technical tasks (image analysis, code optimization)
Difficulty maintaining context across long client conversations
The platform combines a modern full-stack web application with powerful Agentic AI capabilities to automate and enhance promotion and communication workflows.
AI Email Content Generator: Creates personalized, high-converting promotional emails tailored to each campaign.
Real-time Agentic Chat: Intelligent assistant supporting image analysis and multi-file code uploads in real time.
Autonomous Reasoning & Tool Use: Agents that can plan, execute, reflect, and iterate without human intervention.
Multi-modal Support: Image understanding combined with code analysis and optimization.
Campaign Orchestration: AI-assisted planning and execution of full marketing campaigns.
User Input → Real-time Chat / Email Composer
Retrieval (if RAG enabled) from Vector DB
Tool Calling — image analysis, code execution, etc.
Memory Storage — Short-term context + Long-term Vector
Phase 1 — LLM Fundamentals: Transformer basics, Prompt engineering, Role prompting, Chain-of-Thought, Structured output (JSON), Temperature/Top-p
Phase 2 — Embeddings & RAG: Embeddings, Vector DB (Qdrant), Vector similarity (Cosine), RAG architecture
Phase 4 — Tool Calling & Guarded Execution: Tool-augmented architecture, JSON schema, Tool selection logic
Phase 5 — Agent Loop & Autonomous Reasoning: ReAct pattern, Plan → Execute → Observe cycle, Reflection
Phase 6 — Memory Systems: Long-term memory (Vector DB), Semantic memory, Task history (partial)
Total AI Integration Phase: approximately 1 to 2.5 weeks (around 64 hours).
Advanced evaluation & observability — full A/B testing, precision metrics

The AI-Powered Promotion & Communication Platform is rapidly evolving into a comprehensive solution that significantly reduces manual effort while enhancing creativity and efficiency for marketing agencies. With core real-time Agentic AI chat and email generation already functional, the project is well-positioned to deliver transformative value.

This living document will continue to be updated as development progresses. The next 1 weeks of focused AI integration will bring multi-agent autonomy, robust memory systems, and full campaign orchestration online.
This document describes the implementation of real-time Chat and Calling systems using NestJS (backend) and Next.js (frontend) with Socket.IO for communication.

- Backend: NestJS
- Frontend: Next.js
- Real-time: Socket.IO
- Authentication: JWT

- One-to-one messaging
- Group chat (rooms)
- Typing indicators
- Message delivery and read receipts
- Presence (online/offline)
join_room, leave_room, message, edit_message, delete_message, typing, mark_read, get_messages
Client → Socket.IO → NestJS Gateway → Chat Service → Database → Emit back to clients

- Audio/Video call signaling
- Call initiation and termination
- Incoming call notification
- Call accept/reject
- Peer-to-peer communication (WebRTC)
call_user, incoming_call, accept_call, reject_call, end_call, ice_candidate, offer, answer
Caller → call_user → Receiver gets incoming_call → accept_call → WebRTC negotiation (offer/answer) → call established
JWT-based authentication is used for both chat and calling. All socket connections are validated before use.
Use Redis adapter for scaling multiple instances. Consider message queues like Kafka or RabbitMQ for heavy workloads.
